commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Codex/OpenAI-compatible progress-only post-tool assistant detection, continuation retry wiring in `runEmbeddedAgent`, and resolver/runner tests.

PR surface: Source +94, Tests +268. Total +362 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: no. live current-main reproduction was run here. Source inspection and the PR discussion support the path: current main treats non-empty post-tool `stop` assistant text as terminal output, while the PR reports OpenClaw 2026.5.28 replay truncation for progress-only Codex Harness turns.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor and maintainer action: fix the type/replay-safety defects and add real behavior proof before normal review can clear it; automation cannot supply the contributor's setup proof.

target.title: fix(codex): continue post-tool progress-only harness turns
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Codex Harness can currently accept a post-tool assistant message as final even when the text is only a progress announcement, for example:

- `Now let me create the sample logs ...`
- `Now the Flask server with all API endpoints.`

Those messages are not final deliverables; they describe the next concrete step. In replay, this caused the judge to inspect a truncated workspace after only a few tool rounds. This PR makes the embedded runner continue once from the current transcript when that narrow post-tool progress-only shape is detected.

## Summary

This replaces the earlier conflicting PR with a rebased, smaller patch on current `main`.

## Changes

- Add a narrowly scoped post-tool progress-only continuation classifier for Codex/OpenAI-compatible response paths.
- Retry once from the current transcript with an explicit continuation instruction.
- Keep final-summary text such as `Done...` from triggering continuation.
- Add resolver-level coverage for the two observed progress-only shapes and a final-summary negative case.
- Add runner-level coverage proving `runEmbeddedAgent` performs a second attempt and injects the continuation prompt.

This PR intentionally does not touch Codex app-server timeout configuration because current `main` already contains `postToolRawAssistantCompletionIdleTimeoutMs` support.

## Evidence

Passed on current `main` clone:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.incomplete-turn.test.ts
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests       230 passed (230)
```

Also passed:

```text
git diff --check
node scripts/check-plugin-npm-runtime-builds.mjs --package extensions/codex
checked 1 publishable plugins; built 1 npm runtimes
codex  built  9  -
```

Local diff scope after rebasing onto current `main`:

```text
3 files changed, 233 insertions(+)
```

## Replay Context

The issue was found while comparing OpenClaw 5.28 Codex Harness, Codex ACP, Harness with agent workspace, and PiAgent. Two Harness cases stopped after 6 tool rounds with progress-only final assistant text, causing low judge scores despite the text itself indicating more work should follow.

A live external Ark/DeepSeek leaf rerun is not included here because this execution environment still does not have `ARK_API_KEY`. The included regression tests verify the OpenClaw runner behavior directly.

## CPU Note

The same replay showed higher local CPU time for Harness than ACP. The current root-cause assessment is that this comes from local embedded-runner work: Codex app-server runtime, transcript/session mirroring, dynamic tool bridge, and archival writes. This PR fixes the correctness issue that caused early truncation; it does not bundle broader CPU optimization work.
target.author: marchpure
